## Freelance Service Agreement (California) — Brand Refresh 2025

### Preamble

This Freelance Service Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of 2025-01-15 (the "Effective Date") by and between TechStartup Inc., with Sarah Johnson, Marketing Director, as authorized representative ("Client"), and Chen Creative Studio, with Alex Chen, Senior Designer, as authorized representative ("Freelancer"). Client and Freelancer may be referred to individually as a "Party" and collectively as the "Parties."

### Recitals

WHEREAS, Client desires to engage Freelancer to provide professional design services for a complete visual identity redesign for Client’s "Brand Refresh 2025" project, including logo, color palette, typography, and a brand guidelines document; and
WHEREAS, Freelancer represents that Freelancer has the skill, experience, and ability to perform the services described herein; and
WHEREAS, the Parties desire to set forth the terms and conditions governing the services, deliverables, payment, and ownership of work product.
NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the mutual covenants and promises herein, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## Clauses (20 total)

### Definitions

*Type: standard*

1.1 "Services" means the professional design and related services to be performed by Freelancer as described in this Agreement and any incorporated exhibit(s).
1.2 "Deliverables" means the tangible and intangible work product to be delivered to Client as listed in Section 2.
1.3 "Final Deliverables" means Deliverables for which Client has accepted the applicable milestone and made the corresponding payment in full.
1.4 "Client Materials" means all materials, information, content, trademarks, logos, brand assets, and other items provided by Client to Freelancer.
1.5 "Freelancer Tools" means pre-existing materials, templates, know-how, techniques, skills, software, and workflows owned or licensed by Freelancer and not uniquely created for Client.
1.6 "Work Product" includes Deliverables and other work created by Freelancer in performing Services.

### Scope of Services; Deliverables; Milestones

*Type: standard*

2.1 Project. Freelancer will perform a complete visual identity redesign for "Brand Refresh 2025," including logo, color palette, typography selections, and a brand guidelines document.

2.2 Deliverables and Milestone Schedule. Freelancer will provide the following Deliverables (each a "Milestone"), subject to Client’s timely feedback and approvals:
(a) Logo concepts (3 options) — due Jan 30, 2025 — $2,000.
(b) Selected logo refinement — due Feb 15, 2025 — $1,500.
(c) Brand guidelines document — due Mar 1, 2025 — $2,500.
(d) Asset package delivery — due Mar 15, 2025 — $2,500.

2.3 Deadline. The Parties intend to complete the project by 2025-03-15 (the "Deadline"). The Deadline is dependent on Client’s timely provision of Client Materials and timely review/approval. If Client delays approvals, feedback, or provision of materials, the schedule will be extended by a period reasonably necessary for Freelancer to perform.

2.4 Exclusions. Unless expressly included in Section 2.2 or a written change order, Services do not include printing, web/app development, motion design, photography/videography, copywriting, trademark registration/legal clearance, paid stock licensing fees, or ongoing brand management.

2.5 Acceptance. Each Milestone Deliverable will be deemed accepted upon the earliest of: (i) Client’s written approval; (ii) Client’s use in commerce or publication; or (iii) five (5) business days after delivery if Client does not provide written notice of material nonconformity and reasonable detail within that period. If Client timely rejects for material nonconformity, Freelancer will correct and re-submit within a reasonable time, and acceptance testing will repeat.

### Client Responsibilities

*Type: standard*

3.1 Client will provide timely access to decision-makers, brand strategy inputs, existing assets, and feedback needed to perform Services.
3.2 Client will ensure that Client Materials are accurate and that Client has the right to provide and authorize Freelancer’s use of Client Materials.
3.3 Client will provide consolidated feedback and approvals (preferably in writing) and will designate a single point of contact authorized to approve Deliverables on Client’s behalf.
3.4 Client will review Deliverables promptly and, in any event, within the timelines in Section 2.5, to avoid schedule impacts.

### Fees; Deposit; Payment Schedule; Invoicing

*Type: standard*

4.1 Total Amount. The total fee for the Services is $8,500.00 (the "Total Amount"), exclusive of approved reimbursable expenses.

4.2 Deposit. Client will pay a deposit of $2,550.00 upon execution of this Agreement (the "Deposit"). The Deposit is applied toward the Total Amount.

4.3 Milestone Payments. The Parties agree to a milestone payment schedule tied to the Deliverables in Section 2.2. Client will pay the applicable milestone amount upon delivery of the milestone Deliverable and Freelancer’s invoice, unless otherwise stated below.

4.4 Allocation and Credit of Deposit. The Deposit will be credited against the last milestone(s) due, unless otherwise agreed in writing.

4.5 Invoicing and Payment Due Date. Freelancer will invoice for the Deposit upon signing and for each Milestone upon delivery. Client will pay each undisputed invoice within ten (10) calendar days of the invoice date.

4.6 Late Payments. Overdue amounts accrue interest at the lesser of (i) 1.5% per month, or (ii) the maximum rate permitted by California law, from the due date until paid. Client will reimburse reasonable costs of collection, including reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs, to the extent permitted by law.

4.7 Suspension for Nonpayment. If any undisputed invoice remains unpaid ten (10) days after written notice of delinquency, Freelancer may suspend work until payment is received, and the schedule will be extended accordingly.

### Revisions; Change Orders

*Type: standard*

5.1 Included Revisions. The Total Amount includes up to three (3) rounds of revisions ("Revision Rounds") for the project. A "round" means a single consolidated set of requested changes to a Deliverable based on Client’s review.

5.2 Out-of-Scope Changes. Requests that materially change direction, require additional concepts beyond those listed, introduce new deliverables, or exceed Revision Rounds are out of scope and will require a written change order.

5.3 Change Orders and Additional Fees. Out-of-scope work will be billed at Freelancer’s then-current hourly rate or a fixed fee as agreed in a written change order. Freelancer is not obligated to begin out-of-scope work until the Parties sign the change order (email confirmation is acceptable) and Client pays any required advance.

5.4 Client Delay Impacts. If Client requests changes after approving a Milestone, such changes constitute out-of-scope work unless the Parties agree otherwise in writing.

### Expenses and Third-Party Costs

*Type: standard*

6.1 Reimbursable Expenses. Client will reimburse pre-approved, reasonable out-of-pocket expenses incurred for the project (e.g., stock imagery/fonts/licenses), provided Freelancer obtains Client’s written approval before incurring any single expense over $100.

6.2 Third-Party Terms. Any third-party licenses are subject to the third party’s terms. Freelancer will transfer to Client any license documentation in Freelancer’s possession for approved third-party assets. Unless expressly agreed, Freelancer does not provide legal advice regarding license scope or compliance.

### Intellectual Property; Ownership; License

*Type: standard*

7.1 Ownership of Client Materials. Client retains all right, title, and interest in Client Materials. Client grants Freelancer a limited, nonexclusive, royalty-free license to use Client Materials solely to perform the Services.

7.2 Ownership of Work Product (Upon Full Payment). Upon Client’s full payment of all amounts due under this Agreement, including milestone fees and approved expenses, Freelancer hereby assigns to Client all right, title, and interest in and to the Final Deliverables created specifically for Client under this Agreement, excluding Freelancer Tools and excluding any third-party materials.

7.3 Freelancer Tools. Freelancer retains all right, title, and interest in Freelancer Tools. To the extent any Freelancer Tools are incorporated into the Deliverables, Freelancer grants Client a perpetual, nonexclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to use such incorporated Freelancer Tools solely as part of the Deliverables for Client’s internal and external business purposes.

7.4 Preliminary Works. Drafts, concepts not selected, and exploratory work (including unused logo concepts) remain Freelancer’s property; however, Client may internally evaluate such materials during the project. Client may not use or publish unpurchased or unapproved concepts without a separate written license from Freelancer.

7.5 Moral Rights. To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, Freelancer waives and agrees not to assert any "moral rights" (including rights of attribution and integrity) in the Final Deliverables assigned to Client.

7.6 Portfolio Use (Credit). Unless Client provides written objection for confidential reasons, Freelancer may display the Deliverables and project name in Freelancer’s portfolio, website, and marketing materials after public launch by Client, provided Freelancer does not disclose Client confidential information. Client may request reasonable credit placement where customary.

7.7 No Trademark Clearance. Client acknowledges that Freelancer is not providing trademark search/clearance or legal advice. Client is responsible for confirming that any final branding is available and non-infringing.

### Confidentiality

*Type: standard*

8.1 Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" means non-public information disclosed by either Party that is designated as confidential or that reasonably should be understood to be confidential given the nature of the information and circumstances of disclosure.

8.2 Obligations. The receiving Party will: (i) use Confidential Information only to perform or receive benefits under this Agreement; (ii) not disclose Confidential Information to any third party except to its employees/contractors who have a need to know and are bound by confidentiality obligations at least as protective; and (iii) protect Confidential Information using reasonable care.

8.3 Exclusions. Confidential Information does not include information that: (i) is or becomes publicly available through no breach; (ii) was known by the receiving Party prior to disclosure; (iii) is received from a third party without breach; or (iv) is independently developed without use of the disclosing Party’s Confidential Information.

8.4 Required Disclosure. A Party may disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by law, subpoena, or court order, provided it gives prompt notice (to the extent legally permitted) and cooperates reasonably in seeking protective treatment.

8.5 Duration. Confidentiality obligations continue during the term and for three (3) years after termination, except for trade secrets, which will be protected for as long as they remain trade secrets under applicable law.

### Representations and Warranties

*Type: standard*

9.1 Mutual. Each Party represents that it has the full right, power, and authority to enter into and perform this Agreement.

9.2 Freelancer Warranties. Freelancer warrants that: (i) the Deliverables will be original to Freelancer except for Client Materials and properly licensed third-party materials; and (ii) Freelancer will perform Services in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with generally accepted industry standards.

9.3 Disclaimer. EXCEPT AS EXPRESSLY STATED IN THIS AGREEMENT, FREELANCER DISCLAIMS ALL OTHER WARRANTIES, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING IMPLIED WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, AND NON-INFRINGEMENT.

9.4 Client Warranties. Client warrants that Client has the rights necessary to provide Client Materials and to authorize their use as contemplated herein.

### Indemnification

*Type: standard*

10.1 Client Indemnity. Client will defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Freelancer and its officers, employees, and contractors from and against any third-party claims, damages, liabilities, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys’ fees) arising out of or related to: (i) Client Materials; (ii) Client’s use of the Deliverables in violation of this Agreement; or (iii) Client’s breach of this Agreement.

10.2 Freelancer Indemnity. Freelancer will defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Client and its officers, employees, and contractors from and against any third-party claims that the Final Deliverables (excluding Client Materials and third-party materials) infringe a U.S. copyright or U.S. trademark, provided that Client: (i) promptly notifies Freelancer in writing of the claim; (ii) allows Freelancer sole control of the defense and settlement (provided no settlement admits fault by Client or imposes obligations on Client without Client’s consent, not to be unreasonably withheld); and (iii) cooperates reasonably at Freelancer’s expense.

10.3 Remedies. If a Deliverable becomes, or in Freelancer’s reasonable opinion is likely to become, the subject of an infringement claim, Freelancer may, at its option: (i) procure for Client the right to continue using it; (ii) replace or modify it to be non-infringing while maintaining substantially similar function and design intent; or (iii) if (i) and (ii) are not commercially reasonable, terminate the affected portion of the Services and refund fees paid for the infringing portion of the Final Deliverables.

10.4 Exclusions. Freelancer has no indemnity obligation to the extent a claim arises from: (i) Client Materials; (ii) modifications by anyone other than Freelancer; (iii) use outside the scope of this Agreement; (iv) combination with materials not provided by Freelancer; or (v) Client’s failure to use updated or corrected versions provided by Freelancer.

10.5 Sole Remedy. This Section states each Party’s sole and exclusive remedies for third-party intellectual property infringement claims.

### Limitation of Liability

*Type: standard*

11.1 Limitation. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, IN NO EVENT WILL EITHER PARTY BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOST PROFITS, LOST REVENUE, LOSS OF DATA, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT, EVEN IF ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

11.2 Cap. TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, EACH PARTY’S TOTAL LIABILITY ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT WILL NOT EXCEED THE TOTAL AMOUNT PAID OR PAYABLE BY CLIENT TO FREELANCER UNDER THIS AGREEMENT ($8,500), EXCEPT THAT THIS CAP WILL NOT APPLY TO: (i) CLIENT’S PAYMENT OBLIGATIONS; (ii) BREACH OF CONFIDENTIALITY; OR (iii) INDEMNIFICATION OBLIGATIONS TO THE EXTENT PROHIBITED FROM LIMITATION UNDER APPLICABLE LAW.

11.3 Allocation. The Parties acknowledge that the fees reflect the allocation of risk and the limitations set forth herein.

### Term; Termination; Kill Fee

*Type: negotiable*

12.1 Term. This Agreement begins on the Effective Date and continues until completion of the Services, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Section.

12.2 Termination for Convenience. Either Party may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon seven (7) days’ written notice.

12.3 Termination for Cause. Either Party may terminate immediately upon written notice if the other Party materially breaches this Agreement and fails to cure within five (5) business days after written notice specifying the breach.

12.4 Effect of Termination; Payment. Upon termination, Client will pay Freelancer for: (i) all Services performed through the termination effective date; (ii) any Milestones delivered but unpaid; (iii) approved expenses incurred; and (iv) if termination is for convenience by Client after work has begun, a reasonable wind-down/administrative fee equal to 10% of the unpaid portion of the Total Amount (the "Kill Fee"), not to exceed $850.

12.5 Deposit; No Refund. The Deposit is non-refundable once work has commenced, except to the extent required by law.

12.6 Delivery of Work in Progress. Upon Client’s request and payment of all amounts then due, Freelancer will provide Client with copies of work-in-progress files in a reasonably accessible format; however, Client acknowledges that editable source files may include Freelancer Tools and may not be released unless expressly agreed in writing.

12.7 Survival. Sections concerning payment obligations, confidentiality, intellectual property/ownership, indemnification, limitation of liability, dispute resolution, and other provisions that by their nature should survive will survive termination.

### Independent Contractor; No Employment Relationship

*Type: standard*

13.1 Independent Contractor. Freelancer is an independent contractor and not an employee, agent, partner, or joint venturer of Client. Freelancer has no authority to bind Client.

13.2 Taxes and Benefits. Freelancer is solely responsible for all taxes, withholdings, insurance, and benefits applicable to Freelancer and its personnel.

13.3 Control of Work. Freelancer retains control over the manner and means of performing the Services, subject to the requirements of this Agreement and Client’s acceptance of Deliverables.

### Compliance with Law

*Type: standard*

Each Party will comply with applicable federal, state, and local laws, rules, and regulations in connection with its performance under this Agreement.

### Dispute Resolution; Attorneys’ Fees

*Type: standard*

15.1 Good-Faith Negotiation. The Parties will first attempt in good faith to resolve any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement through informal discussions between authorized representatives.

15.2 Mediation. If the dispute is not resolved within fifteen (15) calendar days, either Party may request non-binding mediation in California with a mutually agreed mediator. The Parties will share mediator fees equally.

15.3 Litigation; Venue. If mediation does not resolve the dispute, either Party may bring an action in the state or federal courts located in California, and each Party consents to such courts’ personal jurisdiction.

15.4 Attorneys’ Fees. The prevailing Party in any action or proceeding arising out of or relating to this Agreement will be entitled to recover its reasonable attorneys’ fees and costs, to the extent permitted by law.

### Notices

*Type: standard*

All notices must be in writing and will be deemed given when delivered personally, sent by confirmed email, or sent by nationally recognized overnight courier to the addresses or emails the Parties designate in writing. Either Party may update its notice information by written notice to the other Party.

### Force Majeure

*Type: standard*

Neither Party will be liable for any delay or failure to perform (excluding payment obligations) due to events beyond its reasonable control, including acts of God, natural disasters, war, terrorism, labor disputes, utility or internet failures, governmental orders, or pandemics, provided the affected Party gives prompt notice and uses reasonable efforts to resume performance.

### Assignment; Subcontracting

*Type: standard*

18.1 Assignment. Neither Party may assign this Agreement without the other Party’s prior written consent, except that either Party may assign this Agreement in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of substantially all assets, provided the assignee agrees in writing to be bound.

18.2 Subcontracting. Freelancer may use qualified subcontractors to perform portions of the Services, provided Freelancer remains responsible for performance and for ensuring subcontractors comply with confidentiality obligations.

### Publicity

*Type: optional*

Except for Freelancer’s portfolio rights in Section 7.6, neither Party will issue press releases or public announcements referencing the other Party or the project without the other Party’s prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld.

### Miscellaneous (Entire Agreement; Amendments; Severability; Counterparts; Electronic Signatures)

*Type: standard*

20.1 Entire Agreement. This Agreement, including any exhibits or change orders, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties regarding the subject matter and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous understandings.

20.2 Amendments. Any amendment or modification must be in writing and signed by both Parties. Email or e-signature confirmations are acceptable if they evidence mutual assent.

20.3 Severability. If any provision is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions will remain in full force, and the invalid provision will be reformed to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable.

20.4 Waiver. No waiver is effective unless in writing, and a waiver of one breach is not a waiver of any other breach.

20.5 Counterparts; Electronic Signatures. This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which is deemed an original. Signatures delivered electronically (including PDF or e-signature platform) are binding.

20.6 Governing Law. This Agreement is governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California, without regard to conflict of law principles.
